name: Porto de Corrubedo – path by Faro and Cabo
name_original: Porto de Corrubedo – sendero por Faro y Cabo
region_intro: Hiking around Ribeira offers diverse coastal and natural landscapes. The region features a mix of sandy beaches, rocky capes, and promenades, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Trails often follow the coastline, offering views of the Atlantic. The area includes routes suitable for different fitness levels, from gentle walks to more challenging paths. Best hiking trails around Ribeira The most popular hiking route is Porto de Corrubedo – path by Faro and Cabo, an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete, following a path near a lighthouse and cape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Corrubedo Beach – Corrubedo Lighthouse loop from A Telleira, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.4 km) path. This route offers a loop around Corrubedo Beach and past the lighthouse, with varied coastal scenery. Local hikers also love the Playa de Fontán – Ribeira Promenade loop from Santa Uxía de Ribeira, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through coastal promenades and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking around Ribeira is defined by coastal paths, sandy beaches, and scenic promenades. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Ribeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ribeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ribeira?
A: There are over 55 hiking trails documented around Ribeira, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes suitable for various fitness levels, from gentle coastal walks to more challenging paths.

Q: What are the typical landscapes and natural features I can expect to see while hiking in Ribeira?
A: The Ribeira region offers a rich tapestry of landscapes depending on the specific area. You can find dramatic canyons and river valleys, such as the Sil Canyon in Ribeira Sacra, Spain, with its unique terraced vineyards. Other areas, like Vale do Ribeira in Brazil, boast lush Atlantic Rainforest, numerous waterfalls, and extensive cave systems. In Madeira, Portugal, you'll encounter the vibrant Laurissilva forest and stunning coastal rock formations. The general Ribeira area in this guide features coastal paths, sandy beaches, and scenic promenades.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the hiking trails in Ribeira?
A: The trails around Ribeira cater to various skill levels. You'll find 22 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 5 more difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone, from casual walkers to experienced hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Ribeira?
A: Yes, Ribeira offers several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10501956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corrubedo Beach – Corrubedo Lighthouse loop from A Telleira&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.6 miles (15.4 km) long. For an easier option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31849097" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Fontán – Ribeira Promenade loop from Santa Uxía de Ribeira&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail.

Q: What kind of landmarks or attractions can I discover along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Ribeira allows you to explore various points of interest. You might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061788" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Baroña&lt;/a&gt;, or natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4653902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Carreiro Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal walks can lead you to beautiful spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3494682" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia do Barreiriño&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2250979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caribbean Beach, O Carreirón&lt;/a&gt;. You could also pass by lighthouses like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4797572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Cabalo Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced adventurers?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more demanding experience, Ribeira offers routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31435940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Emigrant – Cabío Viewpoint loop from Santa Uxía de Ribeira&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 20.7 miles (33.3 km) trail features significant elevation changes and provides a rewarding challenge.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ribeira?
A: The trails in Ribeira are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal scenery, well-maintained paths, and the variety of options available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there trails that offer views of waterfalls?
A: Yes, depending on which 'Ribeira' you explore. In Vale do Ribeira, Brazil, the region is known for its numerous waterfalls within the Atlantic Rainforest. Similarly, in Madeira, Portugal, the Chão da Ribeira area features the Chão da Ribeira Waterfall Trail, which includes multiple waterfalls and pools. While the specific routes in this guide focus on coastal areas, these regions offer dedicated waterfall experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ribeira?
A: Given the diverse geographical locations associated with 'Ribeira,' the best time to hike varies. For coastal areas like those featured in this guide, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. In regions like Ribeira Sacra, Spain, autumn is particularly stunning with the changing colors of the vineyards. For rainforest areas like Vale do Ribeira, Brazil, the dry season (typically May to September) is often preferred for hiking.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ribeira?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991725008/porto-de-corrubedo-path-by-faro-and-cabo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porto de Corrubedo – path by Faro and Cabo&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 10 minutes, perfect for a leisurely family walk near the coast.

Q: Can I find trails that are suitable for dog walking?
A: Many of the coastal paths and natural trails in Ribeira are suitable for dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations regarding pet access in protected areas or beaches. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain the natural beauty of the trails.

Q: What are the options for parking and public transport near the trailheads?
A: For trails originating from towns like Santa Uxía de Ribeira or Corrubedo, you can typically find public parking areas. While specific public transport details vary by location, many coastal towns in the region are accessible by local bus services. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and parking availability for your chosen trailhead in advance.
tips: Area of the Corrubedo lighthouse that is at the tip of the cape located in a rocky environment from where you can see the dunes of the Dunas de Corrubedo Natural Park.
The Corrubedo lighthouse is at the tip of the cape located in a rocky environment from where you can see the dunes of the Dunas de Corrubedo Natural Park.
Views of Balieiras Beach, in a virgin environment. Cove-shaped, with a white sand base. With moderate waves, it is a windy and isolated beach.
